title: Fold-safe](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/playground-series-s6e5/overview%22]},{%22title%22:%22Fold-safe) Race-Year and Compound target encodings
summary: Use smoothed out-of-fold target encodings for Race-Year, Race, Compound, Year, and selected low-order interactions to capture stable base-rate differences.
rationale: The run did not materially explore target encodings, while raw data shows large context-level target shifts. Fold-safe encodings can expose this signal without leaking labels into validation folds.
implementation_hint: Within each training fold, compute smoothed means for Race-Year, Race, Compound, Year, Race-Compound, and Year-Compound using only fold-training rows; map validation and test rows with fallback to global prior. Compare against pure categorical trees under grouped folds.
expected_effect: Medium AUC gain possible; confidence medium because encodings can be powerful but validation-sensitive.
risk: Leakage risk is high if encodings are computed on the full training data before validation. They may also overfit exact Race-Year contexts under random holdout.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
